af_score_components.notes: No MCP server. SparkPost (now part of Bird/MessageBird) transactional email API. API keys with granular permission scopes (send, read, manage) — good security design. IMPORTANT: Bird acquisition means branding and docs may be in transition. EU account (eu.sparkpost.com) vs US (api.sparkpost.com) have separate endpoints and data storage — agents must target correct region. Sending domain requires SPF, DKIM, DMARC configured before production. Dedicated IP warm-up period required for new IPs. Template substitution uses Handlebars-like syntax. SDKs in JS, Python, Java, PHP, Go, Ruby. Cursor-based pagination. Retry guidance not documented.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS mandatory. API keys with granular permission scopes (send, read, manage) — excellent least-privilege design. Multiple API keys with different permissions recommended. SOC2 Type II, ISO27001, GDPR compliant. EU/US data isolation via separate endpoints. API key via Authorization header/env vars. Domain authentication (SPF/DKIM/DMARC) required — good delivery security practice.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: SparkPost/Bird status page, SOC2. API stability maintained during Bird acquisition but uncertainty about future direction. EU/US region routing must match account registration. IP warm-up constraints affect new senders. Retry guidance not documented. Cursor-based pagination. Domain verification is a one-time reliability step. Generally reliable for high-volume transactional email.

af_score_components.notes: No MCP server. Split.io (now Harness Feature Flags) feature flag and experimentation platform. IMPORTANT: Split.io is now Harness Feature Flags — branding and documentation URLs in transition. Admin API and SDK evaluation API are entirely separate with different auth — agents must distinguish. Treatment names are strings ('on'/'off') not booleans — agents must compare strings. Metric events for experiments require separate track() calls — not automatic. SDK initialization is async — must await readiness. Environment-scoped API keys. SDKs in Python, Node, Java, Go, Ruby, .NET, PHP, iOS, Android.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS mandatory. Admin API keys separate from SDK keys (server-side and client-side) — good separation of concerns. Environment-scoped keys limit blast radius. SOC2, GDPR, HIPAA compliant. Client-side SDK keys are safe to expose (by design). Server-side SDK and admin keys via environment variables. Harness acquisition should maintain compliance posture.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Split.io/Harness status page, SOC2. Stable API despite Harness acquisition. SDK async initialization requires correct startup handling. Metric tracking is separate from flag evaluation — two failure modes. Retry guidance not documented. Offset-based pagination. Harness integration may eventually create breaking changes. Generally reliable for feature flag management.

af_score_components.notes: No MCP server. Square unified commerce API for payments, inventory, customers, orders, invoices. Personal access tokens for single-merchant (simple for agents); OAuth 2.0 for multi-merchant with scopes. Geographic availability limited to US, CA, AU, UK, JP — international merchants cannot use Square. Sandbox environment with test tokens available. Card tokenization (nonces) required — cannot pass raw card numbers. Order versioning state machine must be understood. Webhook HMAC-SHA256 verification essential. Retry guidance documented. Cursor-based pagination. SDKs in Python, Ruby, Java, JS, .NET, PHP, Go.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS mandatory. OAuth 2.0 with fine-grained permission scopes (PAYMENTS_READ, PAYMENTS_WRITE, CUSTOMERS_READ, etc.). Personal access tokens for simple agents, OAuth for multi-merchant. PCI-DSS, SOC1, SOC2 certified. Card nonce tokenization prevents raw card data exposure. Sandbox/production environment isolation. Webhook signature verification available. Access tokens via environment variables. Strong payment security posture.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Square status page, SOC1/SOC2/PCI-DSS. Stable versioned API (date-versioned). Retry guidance documented. Cursor-based pagination. Idempotency keys supported — safe retries. Order state machine requires correct implementation. Geographic limitations well-documented. Very reliable as core POS and payment infrastructure for millions of merchants.

af_score_components.notes: No MCP server. Stability AI image generation API (Stable Diffusion, SDXL, SD3). Simple Bearer token API key. IMPORTANT: v1 deprecated, use v2beta for new models. Some endpoints are async — agents must poll for completion. Content policy enforcement is inconsistent — similar prompts may succeed or fail unpredictably. Image dimensions must be multiples of 64 for SDXL. Seed must be explicitly set for reproducible outputs. Credits consumed even on failed attempts due to content policy — no refund. gRPC SDK deprecated in favor of REST. No webhooks — polling only. No rate limits formally documented.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS mandatory. Single API key per account — no scope granularity. No formal compliance certifications documented. Inputs (prompts, images) sent to Stability AI for inference. NSFW content policy exists but inconsistently applied — agents may need additional moderation layer. API key via Authorization Bearer/env vars. No audit logs for API usage. Young company with evolving security practices.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Status page available. v1/v2beta versioning creates migration concern. Content policy inconsistency is a reliability issue for automated pipelines. Async generation endpoints require polling. Credit consumption on failure creates retry cost risk. Model availability and behavior can change. Retry guidance not documented. Generally less reliable than established APIs for production content pipelines.

af_score_components.notes: Has MCP server (quality 65). Browser automation with anti-bot bypass (Cloudflare, Queue-It). 90 tools in full mode — IMPORTANT: use minimal 22-tool mode for most agent workflows to avoid context window flooding. auth_complexity_score=100 (no auth needed — local tool). IMPORTANT: Bypassing anti-bot systems may violate target sites' ToS — legal/ethical risk assessment required before use. Dynamic hook system allows AI-generated Python functions — arbitrary code execution security risk. Requires local Chrome installation. Community project with no SLA.

security_score_components.security_notes: Local tool — TLS depends on target site. No auth on MCP server itself. CRITICAL: Dynamic hook system executes AI-generated Python functions — significant arbitrary code execution risk in agent workflows. Bypassing Cloudflare/Queue-It may violate target site ToS and potentially CFAA depending on jurisdiction. Community Python project — dependency audit strongly required. Site-level session cookies managed by browser automation — can persist sensitive credentials in browser profile.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Local tool — no SLA. Requires local Chrome; not suitable for sandboxed server environments without setup. Anti-bot bypass effectiveness can degrade as target sites update defenses. 90 tools heavy for context — use minimal mode. Error handling is basic. Community project with no guaranteed maintenance. Dynamic hook execution adds unpredictable failure modes. Early project with growing adoption.

af_score_components.notes: No MCP server. Valve Steam Web API for player profiles, game libraries, achievements. IMPORTANT: API key passed as query parameter — leaks in server logs and proxy logs. Documentation is sparse and often outdated — community wikis (wiki.teamfortress.com) are more accurate than official docs. Many user endpoints require target player's profile to be public — private profiles return empty/error. Steam IDs are 64-bit integers causing JavaScript overflow — use strings. Game-specific stats require game's own schema. No official SDK. No webhooks — polling only. Rate limits undocumented.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced on API calls. API key as query parameter is a security anti-pattern — appears in server access logs, proxy logs, and browser history. No scope control — single key for all API access. GDPR compliant. No fine-grained access control. 64-bit Steam IDs require careful handling. Valve's security practices for the broader Steam platform are good, but this API design shows age.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: No formal SLA or status page for the developer API. Stable core endpoints but documentation is significantly outdated. Rate limits undocumented — unpredictable throttling. Community wikis required for accurate endpoint documentation. Profile visibility settings affect data availability without clear error messaging. No pagination on most endpoints — full result sets returned. Generally available but lacks enterprise reliability characteristics.

af_score_components.notes: No MCP server. Storyblok headless CMS with visual block-based editor. Dual API surfaces: CDN-backed Content Delivery API (fast, use for reads) and Management API (slower, rate-limited, for writes). Space-scoped tokens (preview vs public for delivery; personal or OAuth for management). Draft vs published versions require cv (cache version) parameter for fresh content — agents must handle. Nested blocks have arbitrary depth — agents must recursively handle component trees. Schema changes require republishing. Management API has stricter rate limits than delivery API. SDKs in JS, PHP, Python, Ruby.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS mandatory. Space-scoped tokens limit access to specific Storyblok spaces. Preview tokens are read-only; management tokens have write access. GDPR and SOC2 compliant. No fine-grained per-content-type scoping. Management token via environment variables. Public delivery tokens can be exposed in client-side code (by design). No RBAC within management token scope.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Status page, SOC2. CDN-backed delivery API is highly reliable. Management API has rate limit constraints for write-heavy workflows. Draft/published version distinction requires careful agent logic. Schema changes cause republish requirements. Retry guidance not documented. Offset-based pagination. Generally reliable headless CMS for content delivery.

af_score_components.notes: No MCP server. Strapi open-source self-hosted headless CMS with auto-generated REST and GraphQL APIs. API tokens (v4+) with read-only or full-access scopes for agents. IMPORTANT: Content API endpoints change based on content model — agents must dynamically discover endpoints. Population of relations requires explicit 'populate' query parameter — relations return only IDs without it. API permissions must be granted in admin for each content type. v3 and v4 have breaking API differences — verify version. Self-hosted means agents must handle server restarts and schema changes. Rate limits depend on hosting infrastructure.

security_score_components.security_notes: Self-hosted — TLS quality is operator responsibility. API tokens with read-only or full-access scopes. JWT for user auth, OAuth2 for SSO. Fine-grained RBAC for user permissions. Compliance varies entirely by deployment — GDPR achievable with proper configuration. Open source Node.js — operator must maintain updates. API token via environment variables. Self-hosting means no shared security baseline.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Self-hosted — no SLA, reliability depends entirely on operator infrastructure. Stable Strapi v4 API. v3-to-v4 migration was breaking — verify version before integration. Content API is dynamic — endpoint schema can change when admins modify content types. No built-in HA or failover. Retry guidance not documented. Offset-based pagination. Generally reliable when properly hosted and maintained.

af_score_components.notes: Official Roblox MCP server (quality 72) built in Rust. Bridges AI assistants with Roblox Studio for game development. auth_complexity_score=100 (no auth needed — local tool, Studio handles Roblox login). Requires Roblox Studio actively open with companion plugin — not a standalone service. Third-party AI tools get read/write access to open Roblox place — privacy consideration. run_script_in_play_mode has timeout handling but duration undocumented. Long-polling architecture — latency depends on Studio responsiveness. No rate limits (local tool).

security_score_components.security_notes: Local communication via axum web server between Studio plugin and MCP server. No external auth required — Roblox Studio login handles identity. Official Roblox engineering team maintenance provides better-than-average dependency hygiene for a community Rust project. AI agents get full read/write access to the open Roblox place — potential for unintended game modifications. No credentials needed. Appropriate security model for a local development tool.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Requires Roblox Studio to be actively open — not a persistent service. Official Roblox maintenance provides more reliable updates than community projects. Breaking changes tied to Roblox Studio release cycle. Long-polling latency depends on Studio state. Timeout handling for play mode scripts is present. No SLA. Appropriate reliability for an interactive game development tool.

af_score_components.notes: No MCP server. Sumo Logic cloud-native log analytics and SIEM REST API. HTTP Basic Auth with Access ID and Access Key — simple for agents. IMPORTANT: Log searches are asynchronous — 3-step process (create search job → poll status → retrieve results). Sumo Logic Query Language (SPLQ) has distinct syntax from SQL or Splunk SPL. API endpoints differ by deployment region/pod — must use correct base URL. Rate limits on search API are strict — concurrent jobs limited by plan. Access IDs and Keys shown only once at creation — must save immediately. Retry guidance documented.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS mandatory. HTTP Basic Auth with Access ID+Key pair. RBAC manages what operations keys can perform — scope control through roles. SOC2 Type II, ISO27001, GDPR, HIPAA, FedRAMP, PCI-DSS certified — broadest compliance suite. Keys are long-lived and rotatable. Key pair via environment variables. Security product requiring high security standards — enterprise-grade posture.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Status page, SOC2/ISO27001/FedRAMP. Stable API with OpenAPI spec. Retry guidance documented. Async search job model requires robust polling logic. Data retention varies by plan — old data may not be queryable on lower tiers. Region-specific base URLs require correct configuration. Concurrent search job limits require agent throttling. Very reliable as enterprise SIEM and log analytics infrastructure.
